headline: Agent harness engineering and production workflow tooling led the quarter while a cluster of training-stage techniques entered as new outliers.
body: The window's strongest current ran through practical agent and developer infrastructure: Google's Antigravity and Gemini Canvas pushed workflow automation and file handling, a 17-tool Claude plugin roundup mapped CLIs, skills, web scraping, and RAG into Claude Code, and a Google Cloud segment broke down the five components an agent harness needs beyond the model itself. On the technique side, task decomposition, ranking, and trajectory data showed the largest rotation shifts among lagging methods, while posterior collapse, parameterization, initialization, model merging, and relevance feedback all entered as new breakouts concentrated in model training and retrieval. Forward-looking commentary reinforced the shift, with Dwarkesh Patel surveying RLVR, test-time scaling, and dreaming as the next training paradigm and the Big Technology summit framing a move from token optimization to outcome optimization.
